title: Fix the Inefficient Trie Node Inserter

statement.markdown: Implement a function to insert a word into a trie. If the word already exists, return false. Otherwise, return true. The function should also handle a single node trie and a root node with a value of true or false.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a string representing a word to be inserted into the trie.
statement.outputFormat: Return a boolean indicating whether the word was successfully inserted.
statement.notes: The function should be able to handle a wide range of inputs, including single character words and multiple character words.

constraints.description: Word to be inserted into the trie

examples.explanation: The word 'apple' is successfully inserted into the trie.
The word 'app' already exists in the trie, so the function returns false.

hints.text: Implement a recursive function to insert the word into the trie.

editorial.title: Optimal Solution
editorial.intuition: The optimal solution is to use a recursive function to insert the word into the trie.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force solution would involve iterating over each character in the word and inserting it into the trie one by one.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a recursive function to insert the word into the trie.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a recursive function to insert the word into the trie.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The recursive function correctly inserts the word into the trie and returns true if the word was successfully inserted.

starterCode.javascript: // Function to insert a word into the trie
